skill.name: clinical-data-cleaner
skill.description: Use when cleaning clinical trial data, preparing data for FDA/EMA submission, standardizing SDTM datasets, handling missing values in clinical studies, detecting outliers in lab results, or converting raw CRF data to CDISC format. Cleans and standardizes clinical trial data for regulatory compliance with audit trails.
skill.summary: Cleans and standardizes clinical trial data to meet CDISC SDTM standards for regulatory submission with full audit trail support.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 96 static analysis findings have been evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 31 high-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference medical threshold values in reference JSON files, not cryptographic material. The 62 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are standard markdown code formatting in SKILL.md and documentation files. The remaining filesystem and network findings are legitimate tool operations and source citations. No malicious patterns, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or obfuscated code was found. The skill is a legitimate clinical trial data cleaning tool.

content.user_title: Clean clinical trial data for FDA and EMA submission
content.value_statement: Clinical trial data contains missing values, outliers, and inconsistent formats that prevent regulatory submission. This skill automates SDTM domain validation, missing value imputation, outlier detection, and date standardization to produce audit-ready datasets for FDA or EMA review.
actual_capabilities: Validate SDTM domain structure (DM, LB, VS) against required field specifications
Impute missing values using mean, median, mode, forward fill, or row removal
Detect outliers using IQR, z-score, or domain-specific clinical thresholds
Standardize date columns to ISO 8601 format across all date fields
Generate cleaning reports with full audit trail for regulatory documentation
Process CSV and Excel input files with configurable cleaning parameters
limitations: Only supports DM, LB, and VS SDTM domains; other CDISC domains require custom configuration
Missing value imputation uses basic statistical methods, not advanced multiple imputation
Does not perform unit conversions between measurement systems automatically
Cannot validate against Pinnacle 21 or other third-party CDISC validation tools

use_cases.title: FDA Submission Data Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare and clean clinical trial datasets for NDA or BLA submission to the FDA. Validate SDTM domain structure, impute missing values conservatively, and generate a complete audit trail.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical data managers preparing regulatory packages

use_cases.title: Interim Analysis Data Cleaning
use_cases.description: Clean data extracted at a cutoff date for an interim efficacy or safety analysis during an ongoing trial. Handle missing values and flag outliers for statistician review.

use_cases.title: External Lab Data Integration
use_cases.description: Standardize and clean lab results from external central laboratories before merging with the clinical database. Validate codes, standardize dates, and detect out-of-range values.
use_cases.target_user: Data integration specialists working with multi-source data

prompt_templates.title: Basic data cleaning with defaults
prompt_templates.prompt: Clean this clinical trial dataset using clinical-data-cleaner. The data is in CSV format with DM domain structure. Use default settings with median imputation for missing values and IQR outlier detection.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple first-time use with a standard demographics dataset

prompt_templates.title: Lab data outlier detection with domain thresholds
prompt_templates.prompt: Run clinical-data-cleaner on this LB domain lab dataset. Use the domain outlier method with clinical thresholds. Flag outliers for review instead of removing them. Use mean imputation for missing numeric values.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning laboratory results with clinically-defined reference ranges

prompt_templates.title: Full pipeline with custom configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply clinical-data-cleaner to this vital signs dataset. Use forward fill for missing values, cap outliers at the 1st and 99th percentiles, and standardize all dates to ISO 8601. Save the audit report separately.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive cleaning of vital signs data with conservative outlier handling

prompt_templates.title: Regulatory submission with complete audit trail
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clinical-data-cleaner to prepare this DM demographics data for regulatory submission. Validate all required SDTM fields. Flag all missing values and outliers for review and create a complete cleaning report with timestamped entries.
prompt_templates.scenario: Audit-ready cleaning for formal regulatory submission documentation

output_examples.input: A CSV file with demographics data containing missing AGE values, inconsistent date formats (2023-01-15, 01/15/2023, and 15-Jan-2023), and an extreme BMI value of 68.
output: Output CSV with all dates standardized to ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)
Missing AGE values imputed with column median
Outlier flag column (DM_OUTLIER_FLAG) marking the extreme BMI row
Audit report JSON file documenting every cleaning action with timestamps

output_examples.input: A lab results CSV with 5,000 records containing glucose (GLUC), hemoglobin (HGB), and WBC values, some with physiologically impossible values like GLUC of 1200 mg/dL.
output: Domain validation report confirming all required LB fields are present
Outlier detection using clinical thresholds flagging the 1200 mg/dL glucose as out of range
Cleaning log showing 12 outliers flagged and 34 missing values imputed
Final dataset ready for statistics team review with all actions documented

best_practices: Always flag outliers for review instead of removing them automatically during regulatory submission preparation
Run domain validation first to identify missing required fields before applying any cleaning operations
Document the chosen missing value strategy and outlier method in the analysis plan before cleaning
anti_patterns: Removing outliers without clinical review - extreme values may represent genuine adverse events that require investigation
Applying the same cleaning strategy to all domains without considering domain-specific data characteristics
Overwriting the original raw data file with cleaned output instead of saving to a new file path

faq.question: What SDTM domains does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports DM (Demographics), LB (Laboratory Test Results), and VS (Vital Signs) domains. Other SDTM domains require custom configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill replace a clinical data manager?
faq.answer: No. It automates standard cleaning operations but a qualified data manager must review outputs, validate against study protocols, and confirm regulatory compliance.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-clinical data cleaning?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for clinical trial data and SDTM standards. It may work on structured tabular data but the domain validation and clinical thresholds will not apply.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my original data files?
faq.answer: No. The skill reads input files and writes cleaned output to new files. The original data is never modified by the cleaning process.

faq.question: What cleaning actions are recorded in the audit trail?
faq.answer: Every action is logged: missing value imputation (method and value used), outlier detection (method and count), outlier handling (removed or capped), and date standardization.

faq.question: What outlier detection methods are available?
faq.answer: Three methods are available: IQR (interquartile range), z-score (values more than 3 standard deviations from mean), and domain (clinically-defined threshold ranges for lab tests and vitals).
